title: 211 - Community Resource Helpline
content: Dial 2-1-1 for free, confidential information and referrals to local social services including food, housing, utility assistance, healthcare, childcare, crisis counseling, and more. Available 24/7 in most areas. Multilingual support available. You can also text your ZIP code to 898-211 or visit 211.org to search online.

title: 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline
content: Call or text 988 for free, confidential support 24/7 for anyone in suicidal crisis or emotional distress. The 988 Lifeline connects you with trained crisis counselors. For veterans, press 1 after dialing. For Spanish, press 2. Chat is available at 988lifeline.org. If someone is in immediate danger, call 911.

title: FEMA Disaster Assistance
content: After a federally declared disaster, FEMA provides assistance including temporary housing, home repairs, low-cost loans for uninsured property damage, and other programs. Apply online at DisasterAssistance.gov, call the FEMA helpline, or visit a local Disaster Recovery Center. Apply within 60 days of a disaster declaration. Documentation needed: SSN, address, insurance info, contact info.

title: Domestic Violence Hotline
content: The National Domestic Violence Hotline provides 24/7 confidential support for victims of domestic violence. Call, text, or chat for safety planning, local resources, and emotional support. Trained advocates speak 200+ languages. If you are in immediate danger, call 911. Text START to 88788 for text support.

title: Emergency Shelter and Housing
content: For emergency shelter, contact your local 2-1-1, Salvation Army, or local homeless services agency. HUD-funded shelters are available nationwide. Many shelters accept walk-ins, but calling ahead is recommended. For families with children, contact your school district's McKinney-Vento liaison for educational stability support. The HUD hotline can help locate nearby shelters.
source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development

title: Poison Control
content: Call Poison Control immediately if you suspect a poisoning. Free, confidential expert advice is available 24/7. Do not induce vomiting unless directed by Poison Control. Have the product container available when you call. Online tool available at webPOISONCONTROL for non-emergency cases for adults and children over 6 months.
source: American Association of Poison Control Centers
